mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0%, it signals tight labor market conditions that historically precede wage-driven consumption growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, reducing equity risk premiums and lifting S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of HOUST and PAYEMS shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across three distinct business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 102 signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which historically correlates with higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns during supply-constrained regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO remains above 102 for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent positive drift in equity prices over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (correlation coefficient < 0.05) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices above a 2.5% annualized rate forces market pricing of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand or remain stable when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, resulting in a negative Rank IC below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside contracting retail sales indicates weakening aggregate demand, which historically triggers earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration of UNRATE and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging past 600 thousand units signal strong builder confidence and household formation, which historically leads to positive short-term equity momentum during housing-led recovery regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are flat or negative when HOUST consistently exceeds 600 thousand units for two consecutive quarters.
